channel.title: Intelligent Driving on AutoZYX Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Intelligent Driving on AutoZYX Blog

item.title: A Bosch Engineer Open-Sourced a Project That Could Change How Every Automotive Engineer Works
item.description: 9 AM. Your project manager pings you: 'We need ASIL decomposition for this ADAS function — get me an updated HARA table before the client review this afternoon.'

item.title: Cross-Domain Application of Harness Engineering in Intelligent Driving
item.description: A systematic analysis of the deep structural isomorphism between Harness Engineering and intelligent driving safety engineering, revealing their shared underlying operating system

item.title: In the End-to-End Era, Is Scenario-Based Safety Evaluation for Autonomous Driving Still Valid?
item.description: In 2024, a shift occurred in the autonomous driving industry that most safety engineers overlooked — yet it may fundamentally change how they work.

item.title: Value and Challenges of Japan's SAKURA Automated Driving Safety Evaluation Framework V4.0
item.description: In March 2026, the SAKURA project officially concluded, and the Japan Automobile Manufacturers Association (JAMA) released the fourth edition of the SAKURA Automated Driving Safety Evaluation Framework — a 344-page technical document.

item.title: VDA AI in QM: Germany Sets the Rules for AI First — What Does It Mean for China's Autonomous Driving Industry?
item.description: VDA AI in QM: Germany Sets the Rules for AI First — What Does It Mean for China's Autonomous Driving Industry?
